_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Respiratory failure due to any cause
procedure_name: Respiratory failure due to any cause (Pneumonia, asthma, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS), foreign body, poisoning, head injury, etc.)
source_files: Respiratory failure due to any cause_Medco.pdf
Respiratory failure due to any cause_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Respiratory failure secondary to Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)
Respiratory failure secondary to Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS)
Respiratory failure secondary to foreign body aspiration
Respiratory failure secondary to head injury
Indication for admission for management of respiratory failure based on clinical signs, symptoms, physical examination, and investigations.

clinical_thresholds.note: Threshold for starting oxygen therapy and a pop-up trigger in TMS
Abnormal HR/Rhythm pop-up trigger in TMS
Abnormal HR/Rhythm pop-up trigger in TMS
Abnormal Respiratory rate pop-up trigger in TMS
Abnormal Respiratory rate pop-up trigger in TMS
If PFT available, pop-up trigger in TMS

preauth.label: Clinical photograph of the patient on bed

claim.label: Still photograph of the patient undergoing the treatment (+/- ventilatory support)
Detailed Indoor case papers having treatment and management
Relevant investigations including serial ABGs (ABG if Available)

ppd_preauth.q: Is HR/ Rhythm abnormal (>90-100 or <45-50/ min)?
Is ABG (if available) suggestive of Acute respiratory failure?
Does Chest X-ray have abnormal findings?
Is Fundus examination suggestive of Papilloedema (where applicable)?
Is PFT available (where applicable) and FEV 1 < 80%?
Is there a history of trauma (In case of head injury)?
Is there a history of poisoning (In cases of poisoning)?

cpd_claim.q: Was there documentary evidence of record & monitoring of vitals (Respiratory rate, SpO2, Heart rate, chest examination, abnormal breath sounds)?
Do discharge documents show discharge/referral criteria (if applicable), counselling on control of risk factors, and post discharge treatment advice including follow-up?
Do documents show a need for admission to HDU/ ICU (+/- BIPAP/ ventilator)?
Is there documentary evidence to show monitoring in HDU/ ICU/ BIPAP/ ventilator?

common_queries: SpO2 not documented or documented above 90% threshold without justification for respiratory failure
Clinical notes missing or APACHE score not documented
ABG (if available) not suggestive of acute respiratory failure
Chest X-ray findings not documented or documented as normal despite clinical suspicion of respiratory failure
Missing documentary evidence of vital sign monitoring (Respiratory rate, SpO2, Heart rate, chest examination, abnormal breath sounds)
Detailed discharge summary missing, incomplete, or lacking post discharge advice/counselling

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB/ equivalent (Medicine or Pulmonology)/ Diploma in Tuberculosis and Chest Disease (DTCD)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for admission for management of respiratory failure only if indicated.
The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical signs, symptoms, physical examination, investigations.
Look for underlying causes in hypercapnia, headache, bounding pulse, tremor/ flap, papilloedema, coma.
Refer to a higher center if there is no relief from treatment/ mechanical ventilation is needed/life threatening features.
additional_information.referral_criteria: No relief OR Need for mechanical ventilation OR life threatening features: Stabilize CAB, transfer to higher center
